Assembly: The assembly iyApaInim1.hap1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and orientation of scaffolds is unsure on Chromosome 2 between 17.72-1933Mb.

Assembly: The assembly ilLaeCoen1.hap1.1 is based on 75x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 9 (39.8-42.7 Mbp) is unknown. The Z1 chromosome was identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Z2 and W was identified by copy number and coverage in the haploid assembly. There was likely a Z+A fusion and a subsequent neo-W+A fusion resulting in a neo Z2.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Echinaster sepositus. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The genome contains large centromeric regions with uncertain organization. We removed 434 haplotypic regions, totaling 142Mb, with the largest being 9.4Mb. We identified and removed 183 contigs as bacterial or viral sequences, totaling 13.3Mb, with the largest being 479Kb.

Assembly: The assembly icPodFusc4.1 is based on 39x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting using ancestral BUSCO genes. Y chromosomes identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (69,975 - 73,068 ~ Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (30,006 - 33,188 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 6 (69,975 - 73,068 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (69,083 - 70,897 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 11 (28,787 - 31,285 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 12 (61,031 - 68,191 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 13 (46,481 - 48,801 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 14 (34,556 - 37,024 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 15 (13,917 - 15,969 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 17 (6,780 - 9,192 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 18 (20,460 - 22,066 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 19 (13,382 - 14,839 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome X (1 - 20,341 Kbp) are unknown.
